title: storage-v3 #3: rewrite Storage::init to write the format-version sentinel
body:
# Goal

Rewrite `Storage::init` so it does NOT create or touch the
`issues` bookmark. In v3, init just writes a single
`refs/jjf/meta/format-version` ref carrying the v3 sentinel.

End state: `jjf init` in a brand-new git repo creates exactly
one v3 ref (`refs/jjf/meta/format-version`) and zero bookmarks.
No jj working-copy mutations. No HEAD drift.

# Why now

Ticket 3 of 8 in the v3 storage epic (`bd98097`). Independent
of tickets 1 and 2 in terms of code (it touches a different
function) but conceptually depends on them — without tickets
1+2, no v3 reads/writes work yet, so init alone is non-useful.
Filed here so the dispatch order keeps the build green per
step.

# Approach

Today (`crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs:868–875`):

1. Create an empty seed commit (`jjf: seed issues bookmark`).
2. Move the `issues` bookmark there.
3. Step `@` off via `jj new root()`.

In v3:

1. Build a tiny tree containing one blob (`version: 3` text,
   or an empty `version-3` named blob — pick whichever the
   detection in tickets 1+2 actually checks).
2. `git commit-tree <tree> -m 'jjf: format v3'`.
3. `git update-ref refs/jjf/meta/format-version <commit>` (no
   CAS sentinel — fresh init means the ref must not exist).

No jj subprocess calls. No bookmark. No working-copy mutation.

**Detection rule going forward.** A repo with
`refs/jjf/meta/format-version` is v3. A repo with the `issues`
bookmark and no v3 meta is v2. A repo with the `bugs` bookmark
and no `issues` bookmark is v1 (existing v1 → v2 path stays
intact).

**Idempotency.** Re-running `Storage::init` on a v3 repo is a
no-op; the sentinel already exists.

# Scope

In scope:
- New `Storage::init` body for v3-shape repos.
- The format-version sentinel ref's exact shape (commit
  carrying a tree, blob name, etc.) — pick something the
  tickets-1-and-2 detection logic can check cheaply (just
  `git rev-parse --verify refs/jjf/meta/format-version` is
  enough; the commit's content can be a single line for
  human readers).
- Keep the existing v2-init path intact, gated by absence of
  v3 sentinel — so `Storage::init` on an existing v2 repo
  stays a no-op (idempotent).

Out of scope:
- v2 → v3 auto-migration on `Storage::open` (ticket 4).
- Push/pull semantics for the format-version ref (it's a
  local-only marker; ticket 5 decides whether to include it
  in the push refspec — probably yes for transparency).

# Acceptance

- [ ] `jjf init` in a fresh git repo creates exactly one ref
      (`refs/jjf/meta/format-version`) under `refs/jjf/`.
- [ ] No `issues` bookmark exists post-init.
- [ ] Git HEAD unchanged across init.
- [ ] `jj status` (when run after init in a colocated repo)
      shows clean working copy with no descendant commits.
- [ ] Re-running `jjf init` on a v3 repo is a no-op.
- [ ] `Storage::init` on a fresh repo + create_issue + read_record
      round-trip works (smoke test that tickets 1+2+3 compose).
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` green.

# Pointers

- Design doc: `docs/storage-out-of-tree.md` ("v2 → v3
  migration" section names the sentinel; "Ref layout" lists
  it under `refs/jjf/meta/format-version`).
- Code to rewrite: `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs:868–875`
  (`Storage::init`).
- v1 → v2 migrator (`maybe_migrate_v1_to_v2`,
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs:892–1000`) is the reference
  for the detection-and-branching pattern.

# Out-of-scope reminder

Do NOT couple the v3 sentinel's exact format to the design
doc's wording — the doc says "carrying a one-line `version: 3`
blob" but any cheap-to-detect shape is fine. The constraint is
that ticket 4 (migrator) and tickets 1+2 (write/read) agree on
how to check it.
